messages.text: Would you like me to build a **Kanban Board** to track the team's work?

**Conceptualizing Kanban Board** I'm now focusing on conceptualizing an interactive Kanban board. I need "To Do," "In Progress," and "Done" columns for task tracking. The user provided documents like `patient_inquiry_and_triage_flow.doc` and `patient_experience_h2_roadmap_spec`, which should inform the board's design. **Analyzing Patient Data & Team** I'm now integrating patient data from the CSV file to inform task assignments, considering care stage, physician, and discharge dates for priorities. I'm also looking into the team members provided (Sakura, Malik, and Adam) to match them with tasks, which is becoming a core piece of the Kanban board design. **Integrating Team Avatars** I'm now integrating the team member data directly into the Kanban board's task assignment features. I'm focusing on displaying their real names and inline avatars. I'm using `` tags for avatar display, and falling back to initials if an avatar URL is missing. I will ensure tasks are realistically assigned to these individuals within the board. **Prioritizing Application Output** I'm now prioritizing the application's immediate output and focusing on adhering to the specified output constraints. I'm ensuring the application name is in the `` tag, and starting with the core functional interface directly. I'm also confirming the white background is present and using the specified M3 tokens, without any local file writing. The goal is to quickly generate the HTML markdown. **Simplifying Output Strategy** I've had a breakthrough! I can now directly write the `index.html` code within the chat response itself, bypassing any file writing. This directly addresses the rule about immediate markdown block output, a huge simplification. I'm focusing the design on the Kanban board structure with "To Do," "In Progress," and "Done" columns. The real-time synchronization will utilize short-polling via `/api/sync/:envId/:key` for multiplayer functionality. **Confirming Multiplayer Functionality** I'm now confirming the real-time multiplayer implementation through short polling, even though the prompt doesn't explicitly *ask* for it. It's good practice and will enable multiple team members to interact with the board. I'm assigning Sakura Okoro, Malik Harold, and Adam Lee to tasks related to the user's workspace documents, to provide a contextualized and realistic initial state for the board. **Populating Kanban Board Data** I'm now populating the Kanban board with the tasks. I've assigned "allocation latency to under 18 mins" and "Integrate room transition map for PACU to Wards" to Malik (In Progress), "Establish digital SBAR checklist prior to ward transfer" and "Streamline discharge pharmacy prescriptions automation" to Adam (Done), and "Develop Live Care Journey Tracker prototype" to Sakura (To Do). This reflects current workflow. I'm also ensuring the design is aligned with Polaris/M3 principles for visual consistency. **Designing Minimal Interface** I am now focusing on the Kanban board's interface, especially how to keep it minimal and functional. I'll need an "Add task" button or a quick inline add form to handle task creation. I've decided to include fields for Title, Assignee, and Column in the add form, all to keep the board's functionality direct, without superfluous design elements. I'm focusing on "Google Sans" font styling, for headings and buttons, and "Google Sans Text" for the body text. **Implementing Task Cards** I'm now detailing task card UI, focusing on a grey-blue column background. I will create these cards within the defined "To Do," "In Progress," and "Done" columns. I'm building interactive drag-and-drop AND button-based movement, and edit/delete features for accessibility. I'll need a synchronization mechanism, leveraging `/api/sync/:envId/:key`. If no data is available from the API, I will default to initial tasks. **Refining Task Assignment** I'm now refining task assignments and integrating real avatar URLs or initials for each team member, like Sakura Okoro, Malik Harold, and Adam Lee. I'm focusing on ensuring each task's assignee accurately reflects the data, and using avatar images or initials for visual representation within the Kanban board. I'll maintain the tasks: "Develop Live Care Journey Tracker prototype" (Sakura), "Reduce bed allocation latency to under 18 mins" (Malik), and "Create digital SBAR transfer checklists" (Adam). **Implementing Avatar Circles** I'm now implementing the avatar circles for the Kanban board, and I will use these initials circles to display the initials. I am updating my function. I'm leveraging `getAvatarHtml(name)` for these circles, which will display these circles where the name will be the title. I have these team members: Sakura Okoro, Malik Harold, and Adam Lee. I'm focusing on Tailwind for a clean look, including "Google Sans Text" for the body and a white background. I will create a quick task form at the top with "Task Title" (text input) and "Assign" field, and "Column". **Constructing Board Layout** I'm now implementing the Kanban board's core layout using a side-by-side design. I'm leveraging Tailwind's grid system for responsiveness, with "To Do," "In Progress," and "Done" columns. Each will have a header displaying the task count and styled in "Google Sans". The column backgrounds are a muted grey-blue, with rounded corners and padding. I'm creating vertical stacks of task cards with white backgrounds, subtle borders, and padding. **Adding Drag-and-Drop Features** I'm now fully committed to a drag-and-drop workflow. I'm adding `draggable="true"` attributes to each task card, alongside `ondragstart`, `ondragover`, and `ondrop` handlers for column interactions, using HTML5 Drag and Drop APIs for a native browser experience. I'll need to define robust drag-and-drop handlers. I'm also implementing a search input at the top to filter tasks, improving board usability without extra visual complexity. I've also double-checked the `<title>` tag requirement. **Validating Data Integration** I'm now validating the integration of data derived from workspace files, as the prompt directs me to seed the Kanban board with tasks based on them. I realize loading them is not possible via frontend, but I can still use these files as an *information source* to develop the board. I need to make sure I seed the board, without needing to actually fetch them. I am going to populate the board with tasks directly derived from these files, which is a key part of the design. **Refining Single HTML Output** I'm now fully committed to generating a single, complete `index.html` file in the markdown block, and nothing else. I understand no local file writing or any server code is allowed. This streamlines the process and aligns with the instructions. I am including the Tailwind CSS script for styling, and am ensuring the M3 tokens are included. The output is a highly complete, interactive, and beautifully designed `index.html` file, as requested. **Finalizing Component Integrations** I'm now integrating all finalized components into the final HTML output, and reviewing the structure of the Kanban board, including drag-and-drop, task assignment, and the search feature, as I work toward finalizing the complete `index.html`. All the required tasks are correctly represented with drag-and-drop, and the `<title>` tag is confirmed. All the data sources are integrated to generate the board. **Designing Search and Filters** I'm now implementing the search and filter bar for task refinement. I'm focusing on ensuring it's minimal and functional. I'm building an input for the search function and a dropdown for the assignee filter. The layout is one or two rows, keeping the design clean and direct. I'm going to leverage Tailwind classes for the design: rounded-md, border, and focus states. I'll need to define the `filterTasks()` function in the javascript.

summary.text:
**Conceptualizing Kanban Board**

I'm now focusing on conceptualizing an interactive Kanban board. I need "To Do," "In Progress," and "Done" columns for task tracking. The user provided documents like `patient_inquiry_and_triage_flow.doc` and `patient_experience_h2_roadmap_spec`, which should inform the board's design.

**Analyzing Patient Data & Team**

I'm now integrating patient data from the CSV file to inform task assignments, considering care stage, physician, and discharge dates for priorities. I'm also looking into the team members provided (Sakura, Malik, and Adam) to match them with tasks, which is becoming a core piece of the Kanban board design.

**Integrating Team Avatars** I'm now integrating the team member data directly into the Kanban board's task assignment features. I'm focusing on displaying their real names and inline avatars. I'm using `` tags for avatar display, and falling back to initials if an avatar URL is missing. I will ensure tasks are realistically assigned to these individuals within the board.

**Prioritizing Application Output**

I'm now prioritizing the application's immediate output and focusing on adhering to the specified output constraints. I'm ensuring the application name is in the `<title>` tag, and starting with the core functional interface directly. I'm also confirming the white background is present and using the specified M3 tokens, without any local file writing. The goal is to quickly generate the HTML markdown.

**Simplifying Output Strategy**

I've had a breakthrough! I can now directly write the `index.html` code within the chat response itself, bypassing any file writing. This directly addresses the rule about immediate markdown block output, a huge simplification. I'm focusing the design on the Kanban board structure with "To Do," "In Progress," and "Done" columns. The real-time synchronization will utilize short-polling via `/api/sync/:envId/:key` for multiplayer functionality.

**Confirming Multiplayer Functionality**

I'm now confirming the real-time multiplayer implementation through short polling, even though the prompt doesn't explicitly *ask* for it. It's good practice and will enable multiple team members to interact with the board. I'm assigning Sakura Okoro, Malik Harold, and Adam Lee to tasks related to the user's workspace documents, to provide a contextualized and realistic initial state for the board.

**Populating Kanban Board Data**

I'm now populating the Kanban board with the tasks. I've assigned "allocation latency to under 18 mins" and "Integrate room transition map for PACU to Wards" to Malik (In Progress), "Establish digital SBAR checklist prior to ward transfer" and "Streamline discharge pharmacy prescriptions automation" to Adam (Done), and "Develop Live Care Journey Tracker prototype" to Sakura (To Do). This reflects current workflow. I'm also ensuring the design is aligned with Polaris/M3 principles for visual consistency.

**Designing Minimal Interface**

I am now focusing on the Kanban board's interface, especially how to keep it minimal and functional. I'll need an "Add task" button or a quick inline add form to handle task creation. I've decided to include fields for Title, Assignee, and Column in the add form, all to keep the board's functionality direct, without superfluous design elements. I'm focusing on "Google Sans" font styling, for headings and buttons, and "Google Sans Text" for the body text.

**Implementing Task Cards**

I'm now detailing task card UI, focusing on a grey-blue column background. I will create these cards within the defined "To Do," "In Progress," and "Done" columns. I'm building interactive drag-and-drop AND button-based movement, and edit/delete features for accessibility. I'll need a synchronization mechanism, leveraging `/api/sync/:envId/:key`. If no data is available from the API, I will default to initial tasks.

**Refining Task Assignment**

I'm now refining task assignments and integrating real avatar URLs or initials for each team member, like Sakura Okoro, Malik Harold, and Adam Lee. I'm focusing on ensuring each task's assignee accurately reflects the data, and using avatar images or initials for visual representation within the Kanban board. I'll maintain the tasks: "Develop Live Care Journey Tracker prototype" (Sakura), "Reduce bed allocation latency to under 18 mins" (Malik), and "Create digital SBAR transfer checklists" (Adam).

**Implementing Avatar Circles**

I'm now implementing the avatar circles for the Kanban board, and I will use these initials circles to display the initials. I am updating my function. I'm leveraging `getAvatarHtml(name)` for these circles, which will display these circles where the name will be the title. I have these team members: Sakura Okoro, Malik Harold, and Adam Lee. I'm focusing on Tailwind for a clean look, including "Google Sans Text" for the body and a white background. I will create a quick task form at the top with "Task Title" (text input) and "Assign" field, and "Column".

**Constructing Board Layout**

I'm now implementing the Kanban board's core layout using a side-by-side design. I'm leveraging Tailwind's grid system for responsiveness, with "To Do," "In Progress," and "Done" columns. Each will have a header displaying the task count and styled in "Google Sans". The column backgrounds are a muted grey-blue, with rounded corners and padding. I'm creating vertical stacks of task cards with white backgrounds, subtle borders, and padding.

**Adding Drag-and-Drop Features**

I'm now fully committed to a drag-and-drop workflow. I'm adding `draggable="true"` attributes to each task card, alongside `ondragstart`, `ondragover`, and `ondrop` handlers for column interactions, using HTML5 Drag and Drop APIs for a native browser experience. I'll need to define robust drag-and-drop handlers. I'm also implementing a search input at the top to filter tasks, improving board usability without extra visual complexity. I've also double-checked the `<title>` tag requirement.

**Validating Data Integration**

I'm now validating the integration of data derived from workspace files, as the prompt directs me to seed the Kanban board with tasks based on them. I realize loading them is not possible via frontend, but I can still use these files as an *information source* to develop the board. I need to make sure I seed the board, without needing to actually fetch them. I am going to populate the board with tasks directly derived from these files, which is a key part of the design.

**Refining Single HTML Output**

I'm now fully committed to generating a single, complete `index.html` file in the markdown block, and nothing else. I understand no local file writing or any server code is allowed. This streamlines the process and aligns with the instructions. I am including the Tailwind CSS script for styling, and am ensuring the M3 tokens are included. The output is a highly complete, interactive, and beautifully designed `index.html` file, as requested.

**Finalizing Component Integrations**

I'm now integrating all finalized components into the final HTML output, and reviewing the structure of the Kanban board, including drag-and-drop, task assignment, and the search feature, as I work toward finalizing the complete `index.html`. All the required tasks are correctly represented with drag-and-drop, and the `<title>` tag is confirmed. All the data sources are integrated to generate the board.

**Refining Javascript Functionality**

I'm now implementing the `addTask()` function and the `filterTasks()` function to enable the search and add functionality. I'm focusing on connecting the new task form to these Javascript functions, and ensuring these functions work. The drag and drop mechanism is already implemented, but is being used with the functions.

**Designing Task Card Refinement** I'm now refining the task card design for clarity and context, by adding the filename `📁 patient_inquiry_and_triage_flow.doc` to the card and also, the initials circle and assignee's name on each task. I am working on the delete button. I am now adding a clear title to the task, with the initials circle and assignee's name clearly displayed. I am now adding the delete button. ```html

Develop Live Care Journey Tracker prototype

📁 patient_inquiry_and_triage_flow.doc
SO
Sakura Okoro
``` ```javascript function deleteTask(button) { const taskCard = button.closest('.task-card'); if (taskCard) { taskCard.remove(); updateColumnCounts(); } } ```

**Refining Button Functionality**

I'm now implementing the `deleteTask()` function, making sure to remove the correct task card from the DOM and update column counts. I'm focusing on the integration of functionality, including sync via `__sandboxEnvId`, `__sandboxWorkspaceName`, and the "team_kanban_board_state" key. I'm also ensuring the sync functions `loadSyncedState()` and `saveSyncedState()` are correctly linked, along with all associated drag-and-drop event handlers.

**Enhancing Task Card Actions**

I'm now integrating inline controls within each task card for assignee changes and status updates using dropdowns. This makes the Kanban board highly accessible, addressing previous concerns regarding complex drag-and-drop actions. The controls will be: Title, Document reference, Assignee initials circle with a select dropdown, and a status selector dropdown. I'm focusing on simplicity, removing drag and drop.

**Refining Final Output**

I'm now focusing on finalizing the `index.html` output, incorporating interactive task cards with dropdowns for assignee and status, replacing drag-and-drop. I'm verifying data integration to avoid fetching files. I am working on keeping a clean and concise interface, directly usable as a Kanban board. I'm also ensuring the correct synchronization is in place.

**Refactoring Card Interactions**

I'm now refactoring task card interactions. I am adding a delete icon and simplifying card actions. I'm focusing on assignee selection and status changes via select dropdown menus. The drag-and-drop feature has been removed for a much simpler user experience. I am using all of the workspace data in the UI.

**Updating Task Management**

I'm now prioritizing the synchronization of the updated task data, focusing on save functionality with each change to the board. The updated list includes delete functionality, along with edit and status updates, via dropdowns. I am working with the workspace data, which is integrated with all the changes. I will ensure all data is persisted correctly.

**Refining Filter Functionality**

I'm now implementing the search bar and assignee filter. I'm ensuring both the search bar and the assignee dropdown filter correctly based on task title, document, and assignee selection. The `renderBoard()` is correctly called with the filters. The function now correctly renders data.

**Finalizing Data Sync**

I'm now integrating final data synchronization, saving state updates after each interaction with the Kanban board. I'm focusing on incorporating `loadSyncedState()` and `saveSyncedState()` for robust and responsive behavior. The complete data management implementation, including UI updates and persistence, is the immediate focus.

**Finalizing UI Integration**

I'm now integrating the final UI updates, focusing on real-time data synchronization. I am ensuring that the Kanban board persists through `loadSyncedState()` and `saveSyncedState()`. The user interface is the priority right now, making a user-friendly experience.

**Finalizing Task Interactions**

I'm now integrating the final pieces, focusing on real-time saving and loading, alongside a highly accessible and usable interface. The final touches are in place for the Kanban board, and data synchronization is working seamlessly.

**Finalizing Complete Integration**

I'm now completing the final checks and integrations. I'm ensuring everything functions as a single HTML file with no external dependencies and proper syncing. The Kanban board is fully interactive with data persistence and a refined user interface. The entire system is working.

sandboxFiles.name: patient_inquiry_and_triage_flow.doc
sandboxFiles.content:
# Patient Emergency Triage & Inpatient Admission Flow

**Author:** Dr. Marcus Thorne (Chief Medical Officer) & Rachel Chang (Inpatient Nursing Lead)

### Executive Summary
Standard operating procedure for optimizing patient triage velocity, ED intake handoffs, and bed management transitions across inpatient hospital departments.

### Key Intake Benchmarks
1. **Triage Severity Scoring**: Implement Emergency Severity Index (ESI Levels 1-5) automated EHR tag upon patient intake.
2. **Bed Allocation Latency**: Decrease median triage-to-bed time from 48 minutes to < 18 minutes.
3. **Care Team Handoff Protocol**: Digital SBAR (Situation, Background, Assessment, Recommendation) checklist mandatory prior to ward transfer.

sandboxFiles.name: patient_experience_h2_roadmap_spec.gdoc
sandboxFiles.content:
# H2 Strategic Patient Experience & Digital Journey Specs

**Author:** Sarah Lin (Lead Patient Experience Designer) & Dr. Jason Miller (Emergency & Inpatient Director)

### Core Objective
Transform the hospital stay journey through real-time mobile updates, transparent surgical status tracking, and automated discharge planning.

### Strategic H2 Initiatives
1. **Live Care Journey Tracker**: Provide patients & family real-time visibility into lab status, physician rounds, and medication schedules.
2. **Interactive Room Transition Map**: Smooth transfers between Emergency, Surgery, Post-Anesthesia Care Unit (PACU), and Inpatient Wards.
3. **Automated Discharge Enablement**: Streamline pharmacy prescriptions, follow-up appointments, and post-acute transport care.

sandboxFiles.name: hospital_journey_executive_deck.gslides
sandboxFiles.content:
# H2 Hospital Patient Journey Transformation

> **35% Reduced Waiting Time**: Accelerating care delivery and digital visibility across all hospital departments.

- **Patient Satisfaction Target**: Elevate HCAHPS care experience rating from 74% to 92%.
- **Family Visibility Gateways**: Real-time SMS & app updates during emergency triage and surgical procedures.

---

# Key Journey Milestones & H2 Roadmap

## Care Stage Optimization

- **Triage & Emergency Intake**: Digital intake kiosks reduce baseline registration time by 60%.
- **Surgical & Procedure Updates**: Automated status board for waiting family members.
- **Inpatient Ward Experience**: Bedside tablet integration for viewing lab results and daily care team schedules.

## Outcomes Metrics

- **Average Emergency Wait**: Down to `14 minutes` (from 42 minutes baseline).
- **Patient Satisfaction**: Net Promoter Score (NPS) +28 lift across early pilot wards.
